content.problemStatement:
You are given two arrays AAA and BBB, both of length NNN.

You would like to choose **exactly** KKK **distinct** indices i1,i2,‚Ä¶,iKi_1,i_2, \ldots, i_Ki1‚Äč,i2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,iK‚Äč such that min‚Ā°(Ai1+Ai2+‚Ä¶+AiK,Bi1+Bi2+‚Ä¶+BiK)\min(A_{i_1}+A_{i_2}+ \ldots + A_{i_K}, B_{i_1}+B_{i_2}+\ldots + B_{i_K})min(Ai1‚Äč‚Äč+Ai2‚Äč‚Äč+‚Ä¶+AiK‚Äč‚Äč,Bi1‚Äč‚Äč+Bi2‚Äč‚Äč+‚Ä¶+BiK‚Äč‚Äč) is **maximized**. Find this maximum value.


Input Format

The first line of input contains a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
Each test case consists of 333 lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and KKK.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äč,A2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äč denoting the elements of AAA.
The third line contains NNN space-separated integers B1,B2,‚Ä¶,BNB_1, B_2, \ldots, B_NB1‚Äč,B2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,BN‚Äč denoting the elements of BBB.




Output Format
For each test case, output a new line containing one integer ‚ÄĒ the required **maximum** value.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 111: One optimal choice of indices is i1=1,i2=3,i3=5i_1 = 1, i_2 = 3, i_3 = 5i1‚Äč=1,i2‚Äč=3,i3‚Äč=5. This gives A1+A3+A5=11A_1 + A_3 + A_5 = 11A1‚Äč+A3‚Äč+A5‚Äč=11 and B1+B3+B5=9B_1 + B_3 + B_5 = 9B1‚Äč+B3‚Äč+B5‚Äč=9.
Test Case 222: One optimal choice of indices is i1=1,i2=4i_1 = 1, i_2 = 4i1‚Äč=1,i2‚Äč=4.
Test Case 333: One optimal choice of indices is i1=1,i2=2,i3=6i_1 = 1, i_2 = 2, i_3 = 6i1‚Äč=1,i2‚Äč=2,i3‚Äč=6.
